insights.title: Reading GateReduction via script requires a timer polling Dynamics.getAttribute
insights.summary: There is no push-based callback for gain reduction from the gate node. To display gate reduction in a plugin GUI, use a timer that polls `effect.getAttribute(Dynamics.GateReduction)`, converts the linear value to dB with `Engine.getDecibelsForGainFactor()`, and writes it to a slider or display component. The same pattern applies to CompressorReduction and LimiterReduction on the Dynamics module.
insights.verify_hint: Confirm that Dynamics.GateReduction is a valid attribute index and that getDecibelsForGainFactor correctly maps the reduction value.

insights.title: Gate depth control using pma node to scale modulation output
insights.summary: The gate's output modulation (0–1) can be scaled to implement a 'depth' parameter: route the gate modulation into a `core.pma` node and use the multiply parameter to set how deeply the gate closes. This lets you create a downward expander with adjustable depth rather than a hard gate.
insights.verify_hint: Confirm that the dynamics.gate modulation output is in 0–1 range and that pma's multiply correctly scales it as a depth parameter.

insights.title: Gate with reduction VU meter: community snippet available
insights.summary: A working gate unit with a gain reduction VU meter on the GUI was shared by resonant (trusted) as a HiseSnippet (tid:1191, 2019). This is a useful starting point for building a full gate effect UI with visual feedback.

insights.title: Faust gate MIDI: gate keyword is reserved for note-on/off, not audio gating
insights.summary: In HISE's Faust integration, the parameter name `gate` is a reserved MIDI keyword mapped to note-on/off events (value 0 or 1). If you are building an audio gate using Faust and expose a parameter named `gate`, it will be controlled by MIDI note events rather than behaving as a UI parameter. Use a different parameter name for audio gate control threshold and route MIDI via a separate midi node.
insights.verify_hint: Verify the full list of reserved Faust MIDI keywords in the HISE Faust integration (gate, freq, gain) and confirm that renaming avoids the collision.
